channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 316 replies — 1264.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 316 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:42 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62101334
>>62100207
Thank you. I try my best to be the best street shitter DOT preacher. We get paid really good and therefore we eat with metal spoons and not with our hands.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62102768
Remember my young Vishnus of the mindchain. THIS YEAR NOMT + JAM go live on DOT (and other major upgrades). With that DOT will position itself to be about 10 years ahead of the competition. The bull market bottom has probably already been reached. Even if we go lower it won't be for more than like 10%. Maybe we'll get a black swan event mid summer, maybe we won't. The next cycle is probably going to be THE SUPER CYCLE, THE GOLDEN BULL RUN. I would position myself in a project that has some utility like DOT. The meme coin bullshitery is OVER. People are going to go back to their roots. There is no more stupidity left in the market. If you guys can't understand that then so help you Vishnu.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62102845
Just realized this thread has been up for more than a month.

62102949
>>62102845
Yes, I've been saying that I'm paid by Gavin Wood directly and that we, the Mumbai warrirors, are eating with metal spoons instead of our hands because of it!

>>62068446
>>62071796
>>62071796
>>62079572

The Mumbai warriors are winning and we're entering a time of enlightenment in Mumbai because of it. PolkaCurry for everyone. Join us

62103152
My Mumbai warriors, my young Vishnus of the mind chain. Remember. This is THE bull run. I hope you are stacking. For this bull run will decide the winners from the losers. This is the GOLDEN bull run everyone has been talking about. This is IT. The decisive moment where the projects that will become the new Nvidia, Amazon, Google. Choose wisely my young Mumbai warriors for reality is not what it seems. If a coin has no utility. If the network can't handle millions of TPS at a cheap cost then there is no point in investing into it. DOT has everything anyone would think of as an important feature to have and soon, this year, JAM and NOMT and other major upgrades will hit. It'll revolutionize what the sector STANDARD is. Soon my brethren we will be called THE CHAIN. We will be a major focal point, a nexus if you will, for the whole Web3 world. We will be the center and the bridge for every other chain. Enlightenment is upon us my Mumbai warriors. Rise my brethren, rise into a new dawn of magic.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62103167
Kafir token!

62104249
100$ END OF BULL RUN MY YOUNG VISHNU MIND CHAIN MUMBAI BRETHREN WARRIORS.

62104293
>>62103152
>>62104249

Not indian.

62105141
>>62104293
Good one Panjali. You will get many DOTs once we meet at the shrine of Gavin, in Mumbai city, the city of DOT, the city of the Mumbai warriors. Yes Panjali, we shall eat with METAL spoons. We shall consume our PolkaCurry with pride and dignity. We won't eat with our hands no more. It is done. We have evolved, we have become enlightened, we are one with existence.

62107462
100$ end of bull run

62107808
>>62107462
75$
For me IMHO.

62109094
>>62107808
Very good my good sar Mumbai warrior sar. Yes yes. We shall eat with metal spoons once we reach the nirvana of 75$

Not sure if you guys noticed. https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview - The APY is 5.33% and 53.14% of the supply is staked. Therefore the inflation = 5.33 * 0.5314 = 2.832362%. So the inflation is basically 2.83% now and I think the 5.33% is the AVERAGE of the last month therefore we still have like 1 or 3 more days until a full month will pass since the 1st halving. Not sure if the halving was on the 13th or the 15th. I know they said PI day and at the same time I'm not 100% if the actual drop happened on PI day... So yea. But what I'm saying is that the inflation dropped from like 8-10% to now 2.8%.

62109790
My good sars, the bottom is in. I hope you are staking for we are going to nirvana (75$) and beyond (100$). We feed the whole village of Mumbai. The thread has been alive for 40 whole days. Let's see if we can get it to 60 days. If we do then the Mumbai wairriors will eat for generations to come.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62110141
>>62110117
>>62109790
Was this you my Mumbai brother?

62110170
>>62110117
not DOT, but yet another bridge exploit in the DOT eco

62110237
Jesuschrist, how can you all keep talking about this dead project? Just get over it. Even linkies have given up

62110621
>>62110117
What in the hell?

62110845
>>62110117
LOL
LMAO even

62111458
>>62110117
>>62110141
>>62110170
>>62110621
>>62110845
https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/13/attacker-mints-usd1-billion-polkadot-tokens-on-ethereum-ends-up-stealing-just-usd250-000
>Attacker mints $1 billion Polkadot tokens on Ethereum, ends up stealing just $250,000
>on Ethereum
>The Sunday exploit targeted the bridge contract, not Polkadot's core network. Polkadot's native token DOT was unaffected.
>The Sunday exploit targeted the bridge contract, not Polkadot's core network.
>not Polkadot's core network
>NOT Polkadot's core network
It was a issue on the side of ETH.

>>62110141
The Mumbai warriors strike yet again. WE SHALL EAT WITH METAL SPOONS FOR MILLENNIA

62111510
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another point is that if this happened on the side of DOT then governance could just revert the transaction. Hackers can only get away with this kind of stuff on ETH because ETH doesn't have a good governance. The funds of EVERY exploit on DOT were recovered. We can't say that for ETH or for any other ecosystem.

It's laughable how people try to portray DOT as getting hacked when in reality the exploit happened on ETH, not DOT.

Look at the picture this guy posted >>62110117. Why are the looking at the exploit on etherscan, if it's a DOT exploit? How come we see "Transfer 108 ETH from Uniswap V4: Pool..."? Do you notice what I mean? The exploit is on ETH, not DOT. And even if the exploit happened on DOT governance would freeze the funds and revert the transactions.

62111757
>>62111458
But how did he gets away minting that much? Who was asleep at the wheel?

62111917
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another thing. The exploit happened on Hyperbridge, on a smart contract, on the side of Ethereum. And Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT. Do you notice how we're basically 2 hops away from DOT?. Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT (1st hop). Hyperbridge has smart contracts deployed on ETH in order for them to bridge between Hyperbridge and ETH (2nd hop). The exploit was an ETH smart contract exploit on Ethereum. If the same exploit happened on the side of Hyperbridge then the funds could EASILY be recovered through either the Hyperbridge governance (which is a copy of DOTs gov system. That's the whole point. That the corechains inherit DOTs functionality, therefore it's superior governance system) or the Polkadot governance.

>>62111757
Well.. Read the article https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/13/attacker-mints-usd1-billion-polkadot-tokens-on-ethereum-ends-up-stealing-just-usd250-000
>The accepted message executed changeAdmin on the bridged Polkadot token contract, transferring admin rights to the attacker's address. With admin control, the attacker minted 1 billion tokens in a single transaction and routed them through Odos Router V3 into a Uniswap V4 DOT-ETH pool, extracting roughly 108.2 ETH across what appears to be multiple swaps at slightly different prices.
He got the admin right to the contract, therefore he could do an infinite mint

62112179
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
https://x.com/hyperbridge/status/2043676775083803057
"
The attacker minted 1 billion bridged DOT tokens (over 2,800x the legitimate circulating supply (~356K tokens)), and sold them on a decentralized exchange.

We’re actively working with security partners to track and recover the affected funds.

Bridging will remain paused while additional safeguards are implemented and reviewed.
"
I guess they'll probably recover the funds.

62112771
>>62111917
>>62112179

Whelp DOT is finished. This is terrible publicity.

Normies won't bother reading in-depth. Just DOT HACKED

62112823
>>62112771
Well, there's no normies in the market... Oh well. Also, normies don't really have any money to begin with. Another thing is that having normies in your project isn't the best thing to begin with. Like, just look at your sentence
>Normies won't bother reading in-depth
>won't bother reading in-depth
>in-depth
So... in a sense you're implying that I want people who have no clue in DOT. That's the worst thing ever. Sure, they can buy in at 55$, but not at these prices. Normies never buy bottom, always the tops.

62112862
>>62112771
I'd guess what you're implying by "normies" are shallow people who don't know what they're doing. If people have no fucking clue what they're doing then why would I want those people around me? You're the average of your 5 closest friends. If the 5 closest people around me can't even write or read then I probably won't be able to do that as well. In a sense this is true. If you're smart you'll understand that every person has something that they're good and bad at. Just take the good and reject and reverse the bad. Again, the normies always come at high prices. There are almost NO normies at bear market bottoms. And this is most likely THE BOTTOM or at worst the bottom will be reached somewhere mid summer. And the bottom can't be far off. Worst case scenario I see is about 10-20% lower than the last local bottom of 60k$@BTC. So imagine a worst case scenario of 48k$ per BTC. But it'll just be a wyck that'll last for maybe a few minutes or like a couple of hours. I don't really see it going below 50k$ though. Probably like 52k$. And the normies won't start coming until like 100k$+

62112999
>>62112771
The thing is. Normies DO NOT decide anything. Think about it. Normies implies that they're in the middle of the herd. The 98% is ruled by the 2%. Why? Because the herd is asleep and unaware while the 2% is awake and gets stuff. People who understand stuff get to have the control.

62113306
>>61932834
Was it just hacked?

item.title: We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant… — 56 replies — 205.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation. What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:35 UTC

OP

We need to talk. And I mean a serious conversation.

What if that was the bottom of the bear market? With all the blatant manipulation, the threats, and the current mess, it feels like BTC just doesn’t want to go below 65k

I'm a perp trader and I'm curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62090678
>>62090672
that would suck because i closed my long at 69999

62090679
>>62090672
qrd on the ho?

62090689
>>62090678
Most perp traders and crypto quants expect to go below the 200 week MA, that would put us around 50-55k. But I myself am very surprised at BTC's resilience.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62091602
the manipulation is the new normal, lmao, better get used to it

62091605
>>62090672
I told you in February that it was the bottom

62091639
>>62090769
If there's one hard lesson that my stupid ass finally learned after nearly a decade of trying and failing to make it in crypto, it's this:
Other than Bitcoin (maybe ETH too), anyone who bought into and held a crypto that then 10x'd minimum their initial investment and didn't sell and is now too far back down in value for a meaningful profit, is a fucking idiot
Now of course there are cases where lucky idiots hold on to something for longer, and get a 20x-50x or more before selling for profit. Lucky them, that shit rarely happens and it's very risky
But still....never. Marry. Your. Bags. Buy into one, ride the wave of new speculators to a 5x (10x maximum), sell and move the fuck on to the next one for another possible 5x-10x
I don't care if the original bag then goes on to 1000x after you sell, motherfucker you still 10x'd your money. YOU STILL WON. STOP BEING FUCKING GREEDY

62091648
>>62090769
i keep telling you retards that polymarkets/kalshi is the new shitcoin but you don't want to listen, keep shilling your shitcoins while everyone moves on, if you can't see how big polymarkets kind of market could become where you will be able to literally bet on anything, you can't be helped, the world is honestly changing faster than we would normally predict and few people have a clue

62091649
>>62091602
>new
lmao

62091656
>>62091649
new as in institutions admitting it or not bothering to hide it

62091780
>>62091396
>its OK babe
>here's your kneepads and hair tie; I'll be in my favorite chair

62091846
>>62091639
>stop being greedy

lmao...no

62092767
It does seem like the 65k support is unbreakable

62092799
>>62090679
She lost about $3500 USD and made it all back with simp donations.

Literally nothing.

62092836
>>62091459
Oh quit being a spoiled spoon-fed brat.

Do it the good old fashioned way of playing the video on one side of the screen while porn is playing on the other side.

62092910
>>62091639
Will keep that in mind next time

62097028
Well, it seems like we simply can't dump below 65k. This might be it.

62097445
>>62097028
i cant believe itttt!!!!!!!!!!

62097513
>>62097445
The strenght BTC is showing is quite remarkable.

62097600
>>62090672
-$4,000
>has a mental breakdown

Lol, what a fucking loser.

62097668
>>62091648
Imagine thinking prediction markets aren’t going to get raped by regulations if they get any bigger.

62097811
>>62090734
altseason was during the elections and only a select few coins were manipulated up after that
if american cattle didn't put an orange retard in power we could have had more rate cuts and a proper alt season in 2025

62098750
We keep pumping, And this weekend there's gonna be an agreement. Right now, btc finds itself in an inverse correlation with oil. A 80k btc during the weekend could happen.

After that, who knows. Once the oil trade ends. BTC will forget about oil and will do something different. Right now, it's impossible to know if the bear market has ended or not.

62098763
>>62090678
i hate myself!!!!!!!!

62098802
>>62098763
It's okay, brother. I'm OP, I live in a little town in the town and I use starlink and I don't know why but my /biz/ id changes all the time.

62098807
>>62098802
>I live in a little town in the town
in the mountains*

62100618
Let's see if we dump this weekend. So far the pattern has been clear, weekend dump, huge pump on monday. But with the peace negotations happening (or not happening in a few hours), who the hell knows what's gonna happen.

62104313
>>62090672
Will you relax? We haven't even seen the beginning of the oil shock recession yet.

62104318
>>62104313
OP here. I thought the thread was archived. Thanks for the pump. Honestly, the resilience btc is showing is very remarkable. If we didn't hit a new low during these las 5 weeks, I think we can consider that the likelihood of 65k being the bottom is very high.

62104325
>>62090769
crypto is dead. SNDK did 2000% last year while btc did nothing. investing is about making money above all else.

62108188
Crypto isn't even crashing that hard. Like it really doesn't wanna dump. Interesting-

We might have hit rock bottom when it comes to btc. 65k support seems unbreakable

62112594
I think at this point, it's not madness to consider that 65k was the bottom. BTC might never be below 65k ever again.

Which is not unreasonable, we were already close to the 200 week MA and the same way there was no peak euphoria during the bull, there might not be peak despair during the bear.

62113171
Well,we are getting the final signal. It seems like the bear market is over. Hope you accumulated enough btc

62113253
FUCK THIS SHIT!!!!!!

item.title: A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman — 49 replies — 154.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:32 UTC

OP

Can someone explain this shit to me? I know that stock market != real economy, and I know that market can remain irrational longer than I can remain solvent yadadada. But isn't it in theory, supposed to at least loosely map to fundamentals and signals?
There is no end to war.
The ceasefire is tentative and unlikely to hold.
Even if we miraculously reach to a conclusion soon:
The disruptions and shortages will last for many months.
A significant chunk of world's energy infrastructure is destroyed and will take years to rebuild.
Shortage of fuel and its inflationary effects will continue for the rest of the year at minimum.
Fertilizer prices will inevitably cause food inflation during the next harvest.
Further shortages and disruptions of aluminum and helium will affect many industries.
And yet the market is incredibly calm and constantly rallying behind even the most obvious "damage control for the markets" news. Or hell even to the most negative news. The DOW is almost back to where it was late February. Why is this the case? The only explanations I can think of are:
We are in the middle of what would otherwise have been a legendary bull run and even the colossal fuck ups in the world stage can't fully blunt it. It's simply absorbing every negative signal and keep pushing forwards with incredible momentum, only slowed down a bit.
The economy, at this point is under total control of a handful of massive institutional investors like Blackrock and other funds. This historically unprecedented consolidation turned it into a fake gay circus. They call the shots, every other player is liquidity fodder for them. And they are unwilling to call the bottom yet, so the show goes on.
There is no conspiracy. Market is remaining irrational for a prolonged period for some reason, but eventually it will react appropriately to current shortages and price accordingly.
What else could be? What do you think? Help this retard understand.

Early replies (first 3)

62112281
AI is fucking here. It is that simple

62112285
>>62112276
who cares nerd, line goes up

62112299
The market runs on fake news headlines now. Perception is reality nigga and reality is looking pretty good! Iran's military no longer exists and now they have no economy. That's what matters. Gas prices, unemployment, and the other real world issues you are worried about only affect the goyim.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62112541
>>62112276
Stock manipulators don't care anymore. They know what's on the horizon

62112553
>>62112276
It is insane and unprecedented.

62112563
>>62112441
>>62112460
People will post shit like this until it actually tanks, just like during COVID where it, turns out, it wasn't being priced in at all. Market is just turning a willful blind eye to the events unfolding, gambling that it'll nothingburger out. But I do have extensive knowledge in one domain that gives me an advantage over other participants here and that's anti-semitism. Israel isn't letting this unwind, that's not how Jews work.

62112566
>>62112491
>I believe my arguments are backed with underlying data and coherent reasoning
And your belief isn't corresponding to actual reality - people who specialize in the various parts of the market aren't panicking as much as you. I don't think the market is irrational just because it doesn't do what I expect it to, it just means that I don't have enough information to predict the market, and I'm ok with that. I'm not being intentionally vague, I don't know the answer either. But I don't pretend to know more than the market, nor do I pretend to understand the market. The market is simply the market. It panics at what I think are the most minor things, and ignores wars that have been telegraphed for months.

62112574
>>62112281
This and none of the currency and debt thay brought the previous ATHs was destroyed.
Debt and currency creation remains full speed ahead.
It's an inevitability that there's going to be exceedingly new ATHs in succession.

62112575
>>62112529
no you fucking retard. The effect of trump tweets lasts like 10 minutes. Everything else is a wide variety of factors you refuse to acknowledge because you are stupid and poor. The guy you were replying to was right

62112578
>>62112563
>it wasn't being priced in at all
COVID itself was priced in, what wasn't priced in was how fucked the supply chains would be afterwards. The US had seen much worse pandemics (e.g. yellow fever, spanish flu, swine flu, and smallpox) and they hadn't caused such supply chain disruptions.

62112585
>>62112276
See: >>62112443
The real detail is that Trump is the unabashed will of the international capitalist oligarchy. Immiseration of the labor/specialist class through AI, shifting of the tax burden off of asset holders and onto the productive economic sectors (labor), economic destruction of Asia through an energy shock (no, you will not be having a very “Chinese” part of your life), and excess population removal through perpetual war (Ukraine perfected the cost model of biomass reduction through cheap drone explosives). Things “go Trump’s way” because what we use to measure the state of things are things that disproportionately benefit the oligarchy (line go up) instead of material well-being statistics (household formation, life expectancy, etc.)

62112595
>>62112563
>anti-semitism advantage
kek. So in your professional opinion, are they really going for it, i.e. the messianic happenings?
Or are the banking jews just trolling the religious jews along with the storybook memes in order to bring in the next post-fiat global enslavement scam?

62112603
>>62112276
Stocks and markets are about PREDICTED not CURRENT, not NEAR TERM, but MORE LONG TERM than that outcomes.
Uncertainty operates mainly on the latter.
Uncertainty is LOWERING right now, even as there is further political Kabuki theater.

62112623
>>62112595
I do think the market is underweighting Israel's intent to keep things moving, and that they don't understand how Jew's works with this stuff as well as I do.

>>62112578
>COVID itself was priced in, what wasn't priced in was how fucked the supply chains would be afterwards.
No it wasn't, it caused a historic crash once lockdowns were announced. But I agree with the latter part, and both of these points contribute to the main point I was making. It's easy to say 'priced in, markets forward looking', but the market only exists because it's not perfectly efficient and these sudden mispricing discoveries happen somewhat often. Two weeks ago SPY was $630 and it was 'pricing in' further downtrend and escalation. Nothing has fundamentally changed since, and things have in fact only gotten worse.

The only question then is whether $630 was an exaggerated movement and fair value sits closer to where we are now than it does down there. I'm betting it's closer to where we were at a couple weeks ago personally. Maybe the market is pricing in a perfect recovery, but again I have the powers of anti-semitism on my side.

62112669
>>62112491
>I believe my arguments are backed with underlying data and coherent reasoning. If not, show where they are erroneous.
You are reacting to the now, you are not thinking like a) the long term investors who thinks 5 years ahead, or b) the traders and institutions who sell to/buy from these long term investors.
You are just a typical scared short sighted retail dilettante (not meant as an attack, it's just reality).

62112677
>>62112595
>the messianic happenings?
>the next post-fiat global enslavement scam
IDK how far that stuff is gonna go, I just don't think Israel is gonna give up so easily on the situation in Iran, Lebanon, etc. US dipped its toe into the pool and Israel is going to do everything they can to draw us in further, especially with Bibi facing trial when back to peace time. Feels like this is his 'all or nothing' gambit unfolding.

62112688
>>62112669
Long term investors also have to react to current events to forecast potential multi-year drawdowns and gauge opportunity cost and risk of longing at present levels. As that risk increases, capital would logically flow out of major indices. The market is also not driven solely by long term baggies with multi-year horizons. Big funds have annual and quarterly targets and obligations.

What see unfolding now is bearish on the next 5 years vs this war not happening at all. That's not really being reflected in price action currently.

62112702
>>62112688
This is hard to frame this if you're not inherently prone to conceptualizing things as probabilities, trajectories, and volatility surfaces/topologies, but: the space of POSSIBLE OUTCOMES has COMPRESSED a lot as of now. At the start of the war, it looked like it might be the case like that there might be a many-month war where more than half of the oil infrastructure in the Gulf gets destroyed, desalination plants blown up, and so on. But now it does NOT look like that MAXIMALLY BAD outcome can happen anymore. It looks like it's become a way smaller-scale affair with A LOT of the potential "branching off points" for that possible future already behind us and accounted for (and successfully negotiated around).
That is why now people are less scared than at the beginning of the war, even after technically some damage to facilities has been done. It is now not possible to balloon AS MUCH anymore as at the start of the way.
Hence, the initial crash and now recovery.

62112705
>>62112276
> 'If the money is not in the real economy it is in financial markets'

62112718
>>62112702
I'm aware of all that, and it's usually distilled into 'the market hates uncertainty'. My opinion is simply that the maximally bad outcome is still on the table, and the market is underweighting the risk of outcomes in that neighborhood (ie, not maximally bad but still worse than being assumed currently).

What you're saying is, again, exactly what I described - 'priced in, markets forward looking' until it violently reprices.

62112750
>>62112718
The maximum negative outcome is everyone looking out the window after noticing a suddenly bright light shines through then having their faces shredded by broken glass.

In that case who gives a shit if you lost all your money betting in the markets?

62112756
>>62112702

Real long way to post “tail risk has been priced to 0”. Cool, I guess, I’ll take the risk/reward of VIX calls 20xing over chasing dogshit like BAKA up here

62112759
>>62112756
The phrase "tail risk" doesn't mean anything to anyone who doesn't know about tail risk.

62112760
>>62112702
I also get really tired of people talking like if you don't *exactly* agree with every nuance of their take, you're wrong. The market exists because these disagreements do, disagreement is innate here. You can place your bets, I'll place mine. The truth is the market doesnt' directly express _any_ beliefs, and no one fully knows what's happening, what will happen and how price will react. I'm fine with discussing whatever but to act like you have this crystal ball that tells you exactly what the market is pricing in is absurd. If the market is one thing constantly, it's unpredictable.

>>62112750
The anon I was replying to was using 'maximally bad' to mean:
>At the start of the war, it looked like it might be the case like that there might be a many-month war where more than half of the oil infrastructure in the Gulf gets destroyed, desalination plants blown up, and so on. But now it does NOT look like that MAXIMALLY BAD outcome can happen anymore.

I still think that, and more, is potentially on the table and not being respected in the markets current pricing and sentiment. But to answer your question directly, yeah you can't really hedge against death.

62112772
>>62112756
>I’ll take the risk/reward of VIX calls 20xing
Exactly my perspective too, cannot believe VIX is so low right now.

62112818
>>62112276
LOOK AT 2008 RETARD. SHIT WAS BAD BEFORE THE EVENTUAL FALL AND THE MARKET TOOK TIME TO REACT. THATS THE CASE HERE TOO, WITH ALSO THE FACT THAT GEOPOLITICAL ISSUES IMPROVE WITH TIME, NOT GET WORSE
JUST BUY CALLS. MARKETS ARE GOING UP AND TO THE RIGHT BECAUSE INFLATION AND DOLLAR DEVALUATION IS IN PROGRESS AND IS MANDATORY SO THE US GOVT CAN AFFORD INTEREST ON ITS DEBT. YOU WILL INVEST OR YOU WILL GET MUCH POORER. 2028 THOSE WHO HAVENTE INVESTED EVERY CENT THEY HAVE WILL BE POORER THAN THIRDIES AND HOMELESS
THE WAY TO AVOID THIS IS TO BUY 0DTE SPY CALLS EVERY.SINGLE.DAY FROM NOW ON

62112825
>>62112276
You're just a sidelined cuck who is mad because the markets are irrational which has always been the case.
You were given a chance to buy last year after the 20% correction but I guess it's not enough for greedy doomers like you.

62112852
>>62112276
understand the liquidation cascade

62112902
You also have to keep in mind that when the US stock market has returned to the same dollar price, that means it has actually lost 10% of its value because the dollar itself has lost 10% of its value.

62112956
>>62112902
'inflation' isnt' the answer to the 12% pump in the last two weeks.

62112973
All that bullshit you posted is already riced in faggot

62113200
>>62112956
No, the inflation expectation is. Right now if you're not a complete fucking retard you should know that sometime this year the economy will start hurting real bad and that the government will attempt to solve it with the money printer and that'll pump the markets. COVID is exactly the situation you should be looking at. yeah there was a big crash, but was immediately followed by an insane gigapump. Even if you bought at the absolute peak right before the crash, you'd be in the money before the end of the year and most likely have made a lot of gains. Knowing that, the rational strategy is to just buy every single dip and that prevents the markets from going into exactly the kind of crash that everyone assumes a rational market would go in right now.

item.title: It costs 300k to raise a kid — 38 replies — 133.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
It costs 300k to raise a kid

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:34 UTC

OP

WTF are normies doing? What are they even spending money on?
Now women refuse to have kids because it'll nuke their lifestyle, while destitute browns have 10 kids.

>buy diapers
>food cost is negligible
>hand me down clothes
>wife does child care
>public school is free

I bet /biz/ could raise a kid on 20k.

Early replies (first 3)

62104065
>>62104059
Basterd benchid donut rase da kid! Shalom rabbi!

62104067
>>62104059
govs keep raising taxes as if that doesnt have a cost. "its only 750 more dollars this year bro! gotta pay for bloated bureaucracy bro!"

62104095
>>62104059
I do believe you also factor in opportunity costs. you support a child for what? 20-25 years? thats money that could have been in the stock market. I child also requires you to move into a better neighborhood, bigger apartment, bigger car and everything else that comes with it. 200-300k sounds realistic over that time span. just because you can live frugally, doesnt mean your child can.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62104153
>>62104059
Every time I see someone with kids I am thankful I'm not them. I can't imagine spending 300k on them and having no freedom or free time. inb4 /biz/ copes about kids being cheap because of "muh hand me downs" and "muh food is cheap". We all know that's bullshit just like your 200k remote do-nothing job and 8 inch dick.

62104255
>>62104059
>food cost is negligible
Lmao
>hand me down clothes
Not everybody has close family
>wife does child care
Sure, unless she has a phone or TV and gets blasted with propaganda to be a stronk independent wahmen
>public school is free
Your whole list could have been summarized with:
>I'm not 18 yet and I don't get it

62104511
>>62104059
>kids
It's
>keeyids

62104518
>>62104059
Usually it costs 800k, but when a nigger is depicted it suddenly goes down to 300k.

62104534
>>62104059
Thats for 18 years you fucking tard

62104581
>>62104059
that picture is AI

62104957
>>62104113
Naturally
A: Women get their first child as teen when the bones can still grow
B: Women eat real food and are not malnoutrished so they have wide hips
And bam birth is easy. You retard think nature made an error? Civilization made an error.

62104970
>>62104059
if you don't vax them, healthcare goes down to zero. homeschool them and they won't care about buying dumb shit because the other kid has it.

62105055
>>62104059
Maybe they cost 10k max

62105057
>>62104113
>moot
Who?

62105076
>>62104095
This. Raising a child when he’s still a 40 year old, kissless virgin living with his parents will cost them at least $300k by then. Parents pray that Skeeter Jean, JiDion, ElizXbeth or Alex Rosen catch him with CP on his phone one day and let the cops send their mankid to jail for life and put the parents out of their misery lol

62105950
They keep inflating these numbers by adding in the cost of college and assuming that additional kids don't get marginally cheaper as you accumulate stuff that can become hand-me-downs. They also assume you're a total goy-cattle and buy everything from clothes to baby toys brand new.

Violently reject the anti natal propaganda

It has never been easier for you to have an outsized impact on the demographic and genetic future of the species than now. US total fertility is 1.57 on average. Globally, the only populations that are technologically capable and not in TFR freefall are Israelis (including the secular ones), the Nordics, and Anglo settler states (USA/CAN/AU/NZ). These are the only population groups worth betting on or forming business ventures with. Because they are the only ones that are going to matter.

Get your genes onto the orbital habitats.

62106191
>>62104059
So new dad with a 1.5 yr old.
Kid expenses:
>0-6 months
Food: $0 (only breastmilk)
Clothes: $150 (once upon a child for discount clothes and toys)
Toys: $100
Carrier: $0 (family friend's old one)

>6-12 months
Food: maybe like a $100? (She would eat like half a cucumber or three chocolate chips for a "meal" + breast milk)
Clothes: $200 (bigger = more fabric = little more expensive)
Toys: $200 (also relatives gift a lot if toys so not bad
Carrier: $150 (size up)

12-16 months
Food: 300? Idk, still not much at all
Clothes: $400 (i got her a bunch of non-discount stuff because i thought it looked cute, not a necessary expense)
Toys: 150
Carrier: same size

62106346
>>62104123
/biz/ makes me feel sane. I was also thinking the same thing.

>childhood friend gets his first gf at 29
>married and had a baby within a year of meeting her
>got her pregnant while she was finishing her masters
>fast forward 2 years
>son has autism and some heart valve problem
>wife has never had a job at 28
>she plans to stay home with the son for another year, at least
>meanwhile my friend works like 60 hours a week making like 3500 euros a month
>needs to smoke, drink and binge shitty online games to keep up with the stress
>she doesn't even cook, they live in walking distance from her mom so she just eats there like 3-4 times a week with the kid and my friend has to order food or cook for himself


All I can think of is "what the actual fuck?"

62106420
>>62106191
R u a retard? You forgot diapers

62106481
>>62106346
>wife has never had a job at 28
my sister leaves her child at her/our parents for the majority of the time even though she is physically fit and earns really good money. the child is already 13 and as far as I can tell he considers his grandmother as his actual mother. despite all that she still makes demands on how to raise the child. I swear to fucking god modern women/mothers are cooked. everywhere you go you see mothers on their fucking smartphone instead of interacting with their child, let alone giving them internet/social media access early on. this is a lost generation. I look at my nephew and I get somewhat scared, not because of him but because I realize his generation has to build/main the world in the next 20 or so years and they are nowhere equipped to do so.

62106774
>>62104104
Jews

62106784
>>62104059
Damn. Every brownoid has millions to spend on kids.
>inb4 their spending our money
I know.

62107157
>>62104059
>wife does child care
heh

62107809
Literally just shop at costco this isn't hard.

62107817
>>62104059
All the cost are negligible other than daycare and sports even diapers. Anti-natalist propaganda is at its core evil unless youre a gnostic.

62107842
>>62104059
300k for their entire childhood? Good deal. I'm having one.

62107999
It's 300k unless you're black in which case it's free because they abandoned the kid.

62108026
>>62104511
eyy, yo whiteboi. joo know where joo at? stop trya dizz owo cullchure and shiiet

62110032
>>62106420
Cloth diapers + diapers cost fucking nothing. It is like 40 cents a diaper at costco and she wears like 5 a day max because cloth diapers exist and you just chuck those in the wash

62112185
>>62110032
Reminder that modern washing machines and detergents are not designed for cleaning and sanitizing shit soils properly

62112201
Is this the demoralization thread?

62112254
>>62104957
this
women of the past would pop out 12 children and then live until their 80s
paleolithic women were built like brick shit houses and were over 6ft tall with wide hips, toned bodies and big booba

62112450
>>62104059
what prompt use to get this? impossible to find real life imagery like this. fascinating!

62113247
>>62104059
Its free if youre a Black King.

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:40 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62095857
fuck stinks
hate them all
their dicks are fucking small
fuck stinks
hate them all
their token's price will fall
it really is that simple

62098788
soon …

62098818
>>62095845
>A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking.
How schizo must one be to believe this shit?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62099283
>>62095845
>basing your financial decisions based on some imaginary income that doesn't even exist

62099287
>>62095845
Didn't read, only came here to mock you and ch*inlink.

kekbaggies.

62099288
fuck every single stinkie
hate em all forever
think they're worthless and dumb yeah

62099312
>>62098788
>Sergey is the only one of all of them who ascended enough to break out to freedom.
Checks out.

62099425
Link fuddies will be tortured mercilessly and kept as pets/trophies

62099443
Link is gonna end up like this. Forgotten.

62099448
>>62099425
oh i'm so intimidated
except, for you to achieve this sick reality, first you would have to 'le win'
i.e. $1000 link
and we all know that's never gonna happen
so you lose
the end
gg
no
re

62100690
After you've made it with Chainlink, what will you tell people when they ask , 'How are you so rich?'

62100718
>>62099312
That’s anon donning the flannel. It’s symbolic you twunt.

62100787
>>62100690
Who are these people? I don’t talk to anyone so why are they asking me these questions??

62101143
the constant link psyops tell you everything you need to know.

62101887
daily reminder: fudders are shitposters

62101938
>>62101143
>>62101887
it's just every day investors calling out a shitty investment when they see one
nothing more

62101990
>>62101938
>t. gaslighting tg brownoid

62101992
>>62095845
You will reap the rewards alright linkie.

62102182
>>62095845
WHERE IS SERGAY

62102192
>>62095845
staking is literally a pyramid scheme. You can do it, but it doesn't make your 1s and 0s magically more viable as a potential use-case.

whoever said that you could make that kind of money from links is a retard, and you're a retarded faggot for believing them. Sell everything, and put all your money into JEPQ on wall st, you aren't smart enough to do this shit without losing everything to some 2bit con artist.

62102452
>>62095845
>you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking.
If this happens, I will cut off my dick and eat it on livestream. For real, no cap.

62102603
>>62102452
That would mean that the Community staking pool would receive $27.375B a year to distribute. That's not farfetched if we expect the Chainlink Network to collectively process 1 Quadrillion dollars in transactions (i.e. more than the DTCC) a year. That's 3.65*10^-3 % of the transactions. Suppose Chainlink takes 0.01% of the Quadrillion dollars as revenue for the network, 10^-4 *10^12 = 10^8. yeah we're short to be able to pay out to the staking pool which would require $27.375*10^9. Might have to cut that down to 10 cents a link a day.

62105169
>>62102603

I would be more than happy with even 10 cents a day.

ps how much for a gf like pic related ?

62106377
>>62105169
go to a mental asylum, you'll probably find an older version of one. She's got soo many red flags you should be able to hear her, or the drama surrounding her, before she even appears.

She'll also ruin your life. If you're not white, just go to south east asia and find a nice girl there, one who doesn't whore herself out immediately to you of course, with a nice family.

62106886
>>62095845
If BTC shits the bed now, $5 is guaranteed.

62107743
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62108890
>>62095857
1k eoy check em

62108901
Why did this thread trigger the fuddies so much?

62108997
>>62108901
>SELL

62113296
>>62108997

62113287

62113303

>>62113223

62113334
>dude, chainlink
nobody cares, stinkie
when are you gonna realize that it's OGRE
you LOST the GAME of LIFE
hang your head in shame
your ancestors certainly are

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: >deflation is le bad — 4 replies — 10.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>deflation is le bad

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:29 UTC

OP

>but if deflation is bad how come pple is the most valuable company when phone prices have deflated 50% over 25 years?
>it just is, okay
Why hasn't TV-manufacturers gone out of business? There's no way a deflationary economic model is long term sustainable, is there?
Even the price of software has gone down 75%. Why is Microsoft still valued so high?

Early replies (first 3)

62113176
>>62113170
Trump made everything more expensive

62113177
>>62113170
apple phone prices go up every year.

62113208
>>62113177
The way they measure inflation for phones is retarded. You think: it’s just the price, right?

No. They measure bullshit like “new features” like a barely upgraded camera and argue that: “well, it’s not really the same phone. It’s improved. Yes it’s gone up in price but the features are actually enhancing the phone well beyond what that price increase suggests. Thus, it’s really deflating if you think about it.” So whenever apple does literally anything to change their phone, the inflation is reduced despite the price increasing.

Not even making it up. It’s fucking retarded, and OP is retarded for using cellphones as an example of deflation. This is also by design. You know why inflation feels way higher than 3.2%? Goodhart put it well when he argued that a metric becomes useless once it becomes the target because, in that event, everyone fucks with the data to make the target. The way in which CPI is calculated is nonsensical on purpose. Don’t even get me started on how they assess housing…

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62113212
>>62113208
Yep. And if something gets worse they ignore it.

item.title: any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management… — 76 replies — 277.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management…

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:35 UTC

OP

any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job
I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management experience but im not getting any offers on my applications
Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718
thanks for the response anon
Did you use indeed or pay for a website that specializes in WFH jobs like flexjobs etc? I'm wondering if indeed is just all scams at this point and if it might be worth it to pay a job finding website to filter the garbage

62084746
There are no jobs anymore. 200,000 people have been laid off in the last six months while job creation is negative. 90% of job openings go to immigrants anyway.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62097830
>>62085875
>>62085904
>>62085904
>>62087410
Correct. It is a gay fake job suitable for DEI hires.
You need to do hard-core real work as a white man. Cyber is now where companies stick their women and blacks.

62097875
>>62084718
This was me but moved to Poland on 125k USD / yr.

>>62084729
There are tons of remote job sites and even lang specific ones. I shifted to golang and can't imagine anyone works from the office in this lang.

62098209
>>62097875
>Poland on 125k USD / yr.
Damn, baller. Nobody in the U.S. would even interview me for remote cybersecurity jobs unless I was in certain states.

62100225
>>62097830
>it’s for females and minorities
That’s probably why your gay ass works in cyber security

62103272
>>62084678
Bump

62103921
>>62084678
I was on Fiver years ago, got banned after I agreed with a client to just work with them over email, and ive done it ever since.

62104035
>>62084678
I work in tech and not for public companies. Startup unicorns are still usually wfh

62104258
there aren't many positions where you can "work from home" and do shit all every day. your best bet is to apply to chainlink labs

62104775
>>62103921
How did they catch you breaking the rules?

62104981
>>62104775
Fiver scans all messages on their platform between you and clients to check if youre trying to pay without going through fiver

62105685
>>62104981
Can you just exchange socials and then setup the deal that way?

62105877
>>62105685
Bannable.

62106174
>>62104258
I thought you guys said chain link labs only hires pink hair HR ladies?

62106213
>>62084678
WFH "chads" are either larping or blind to how disposable they are seen

I highly suggest you stay out of jobs with that gimmick because HR salivates at the idea at eliminating any person who gets paid decently with some offshore alien. And to make matters worse theyll cut your hours and underhire intentionally so theres a mountain of shit to do while you fufill their harsh metrics.

The lack of commute is nice dont get me wrong(and I can especially imagine that if you live in a shithole like NYC or Cali) but it isnt worth just how unsecure your job is and also how utterly demanding it gets.

T. Worked 2 WFH jobs. The common theme between the two of them is that there was always a tremendous assload of work whenever I started shift and work itself was so aggressive I never wanted to do OT. I quit the second one in particular because during lull months for the company they cut my hours. When I started doing inperson work shit got a lot less stupid and I was ok working OT again.

62106222
>>62106213
Lack of commute is nice in shitholes like ATL, Dallas, or every other state in the country. Name one state that you would love to commute in

62106326
>>62106222
I think youre missing the point by asking that. It IS nice but not nice enough to put up with the rest of the garbage that comes with WFH. They are not jobs youre gonna go 20+ years with and retire on nor have that wonderous "dude I only do 4 hours of work a day in my pajamas and get paid for 12!" For long if at all. They are 100% either looking to get rid of your ass or practically turn you into a seasonal worker.

62106349
>>62106213
I *AM* the offshore alien. Work for an american Fortune 500 company. Full WFH and paid double the national average here in East Europe.

I remember being worried when Trump first became president that he will eliminate these jobs LOL

62106374
>>62097830

>apply for cybersec job
>mark black in the application
>don’t turn on your webcam during interview
>or use AI to make yourself black on screen

Why wouldn’t it work lol

62106382
>>62087410

This. Cybersec is a scam / DEI busywork that goes to (usually military) lunchtime rowdies in DC and Virginia, or foids. I got security+, cysa, pentest, network+, CEH etc, and a meme bachelors degree in cybersec back in 2022 and still haven’t gotten a job. Got about one offer since then, and it was a shit position for like $50k in the deep south. It’s also gatekept like hell. Even the reddit tier lefty white male hiring managers won’t hire you

Meanwhile you see foids and nogs on youtube, tiktok and linkedin bragging about their cushy $80k - $100k+ do-nothing cyber jobs and they have like one preliminary cert

62106486
>>62106174
yes, hence the recommendation. still not a woman, but wfh

62106871
>>62106326
Where are these non shithole cities anon

62106912
>>62106213
this only applies if you're in a low skill or white collar factory worker job (i.e., codetranny.) i work in a niche financial reporting role at a megacorp. fully remote and promoted several times to manager level roles despite the rest of my team being hybrid in another state. i'm not saying i'm indispensable or that no one could possibly do my job. i'm just saying that i have enough "high stakes" responsibility that i am immune to threats of RTO or offshoring.

62107877
>>62106382
>I can’t compete with minorities and women
Sounds like a you problem because I get offered jobs just because I’m white male and they hire mostly women so now I’m the DEI hire
Some of you guys just need better social skills and to lose some weight

62110424
>>62106912
Based and comfy

62112915
>>62084678
>>62084729
>>62084791
Upwork. I breezed through the coof pandemic thanks to freelance writing gigs.
If you want a cert, get an internationally recognized one.

62112917
>>62106486
You will never be a pink haired woman

62112921
>>62107877
What a cunt you are. You asshole nigger.

62112926
>>62112921
How much fried food have you eaten today?

62113047
>>62112926
A greater than zero value.

62113252
>>62105877
Lol

item.title: Average rent in the US is $1,740 a month. — 64 replies — 129.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Average rent in the US is $1,740 a month.

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-04-13 22:21 UTC

OP

Average rent in the US is $1,740 a month. You have to work 87 hours of a 160 hour work month to make that on $20 an hour. Then you have to pay for food, and fuel, and insurance, and utilities...No wonder everyone is so poor

Early replies (first 3)

62105140
>>62105137
Quiet paypigs, get back to work

62105144
>>62105137
i had a roommate for 5 years and now i have my own house.

62105148
I am literally medically retard and I make $39/h. Any non retard person should be make at least $60/h, is not that hard if you are not retard.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62106244
>>62105188
That was me for the earlier part of my 20s, will be 27 soon. It really isn't worth it unless you do it for years, get a house from it or make an insane amount of money hourly to justify it.

62106250
>>62105737
>>62105253
My roommates are Nogs and one seems to be synchronized to my bowel schedule so he always beats me to the shitter naturally like 15 mins before I have to go and I have to enter his worm domain expansion. It fucking sucks bros. 37 and can't flush the toilet properly. Always uses my toilet paper. Leaves it a mess and the outside area a mess. Asked me for $5 the other day, needed it for his Uber eats. Better than being homeless I guess.

62106267
>>62106250
grim

62106354
>>62105137
I voted for this

62106373
>>62106196
>>62106203
>No such thing as shitty overpriced areas
Lol&lmao

Anon did you know by any chance that apartments commonly like to require you to make 3x the rent? So for my pic for example in the middle of some worthless fucking city unironically wants you to make 6 grand a month to move in?

If nots not shitty and overpriced youre fucking retarded and I shudder because you probably habve a car mortgage.

62106395
>>62106373
Overpriced how? Landlords ask that much cause people make that much.

62106473
>>62106395
>landlords ask that much because people make that much
How about charging for a rate based on expenses instead of literal rent seeking? Its too bad inflation for a century+ has everyone scrambling to understand how much USD is (not) worth for goods and services anymore

62106522
>>62106250
Wtf...

My roommate is based and has almost the exact personality as me. Neither of us want people in our apartment. We're both organized and clean. We both like similar music. We both cook and neither of us care if the other uses ingredients the other bought, because if one of us cooks, we just cook for both of us. We both go to the gym. And we work opposite shifts, so one of is is always home.

62106553
>>62105137
This is the lament of a woman who just hit the wall. She is seeing how empty her party life has been, her unfulfilling make-believe HR job isn't the thrilling career feminism promised her, a diminishing number of guys are willing to buy her dinner, she is starting to get seriously fat or wrinkled or both. She is seeing how her life choices have driven her into a cul-de-sac, how her golden years are over, and now she wants to blame "the economy". Weep your heart out, strong, independent woman.

62106632
>>62105873
greetings dipshit, you still receive a lot less than you would've during your parents time. congrats, you're a high ranking peasant in boomerville.

62106640
>>62105190
>live with random people youve never met before
DUDE JUST SIGN A JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE LEASE AGREEMENT WITH TOTAL STRANGERS LMAO

62106644
>>62105873
overnight travel for work is the most humiliating wagecuckery imaginable.

62106672
I thought paying rent is based and totally the financial better option? Oh no no

62106676
>>62106672
it is. op is just a women with their usual girl math problems.

62106709
>>62106672
depends on your lifestyle and goals. if you're a high earning sperg it makes more sense to rent because the comparison is 1 bedroom rent vs 3-4 bedroom house with a mortgage. if you're a sperg what are you doing with the extra 2-3 bedrooms? nothing. you're better off investing the difference between $1700/mo rent vs. $2500/mo mortgage.

62106823
>>62106250
that's hilarious and tragic anon, good luck out there

62106904
>>62105137
You shouldn't consider moving out of your parents home until your earning at least $50/hr. I earn $95k/yr but I still sleep in my childhood bedroom.

62107038
>>62106904
>I don't wanna grow up I'm a toys-r-us kid
Stunning and brave anon. Your parents must be proud.

62107382
why do they allow these threads on both biz and pol? no one cares about the content, so why? just prune them. it will make the board a better place. there's an entire board for humanities and economics where they can discuss it endlessly. why here?

62107569
>>62105149
>US salaries are 150K+ so who the fuck cares?
People with degrees, even technical ones, are lucky to get $50k a year jobs at the moment. People stop letting social media rape your mind.

62108168
>>62105137
just get a high-paying remote job, and this won't be a problem

62108171
>>62107569
>>People with degrees, even technical ones, are lucky to get $50k a year jobs at the moment.
skill issue

62108205
>>62106250
Yo anon, can I get like 5 dollars bro?

62112593
I used to think the system was truly unfair and there was no way to get ahead until I saw how little effort the average person puts into learning marketable skills and financial literacy. Even when I made 55k/yr I still saved a good chunk of my income by not buying stupid shit. I make over double that now and never fell for the lifestyle creep so I still save about 50% after mortgage. Qrd: the average normie is a fucking retard who will kvetch and squeal in order to place zero blame on themselves

62112976
>>62107569
>how little effort the average person puts into learning marketable skills
Ive never seen that at all. Its just that people dont want to do free labor and dodge the fuck out of the mess that is our schooling system. I mean look at this shit, if you have zero interest in being a code monkey a lot of this is just worthless garbagebin demoralization.

>>62107569
>People stop letting social media rape your mind.
TRVTH. Ive seen many a people make their lives get extremely uncomfortable trying to get their number to go up and god damn is it sad.

62113027
>>62112593
most humble anon on this board

62113031
>>62105148
>>62105642
Why do you retards conveniently never state what job title/field you're working in and whether you're in a HCOL or not?

62113098
Why complain about home owners and not amazon? Dumb ho

62113169
>>62113031
Because they are ALWAYS lying.

62113182
>>62106522
I have news for you, bud.

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 93 replies — 68.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-04-13 21:30 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62102223
>>62100192
Ledger is cool

62102233
>>62100530
Wtf is that

62102249
>>62101348
I see aave v3 is better

62102256
>>62100233
Shylink shitlink

62102324
>>62102223
Yeah and being able to interact with DeFi there with millions of users while keeping self custody makes everything feel a lot safer and more seamless.

62102341
>>62091734
You should have ordered healthy foodies kek

62102346
>>62102233
a teenager

62102364
>>62102249
V3 is solid. V4 just went live a few weeks ago. Deposits are still ramping up. Kinda pointless comparing that to years of development. Give it time anon.

62102377
>>62095458
reread it anon

62102716
>>62087997
I made this same mistake in the last cycle. Trust me, all the liquidity will go into BTC, so it's better to buy and keep it on TBVs for yield instead of going for alts

62103228
>>62097432
The smuggest are newfags because they're trying to build their own conviction by mimicking the toxic maximalsm during the fork wars. The oldfags have either all left or have the ability to laugh at one's own expense. Bitcoin still hasn't done anything meaningful and "orange pilling" your retarded friends doesn't help. I consider myself a closet maxi because I do believe in bitcoin but I'd never associate with them or go to a meetup/conference.

62103805
>>62095600
For many bullish reasons

62103981
>>62095620
And are you expecting what then?

62105081
>>62100014
Depends on your goal.

62105096
I DCA into Bitcoin. It can either go up or it can either go down. I'd rather take my chances than holding gold for 30 years for a +1k profit

62106216
>>62095867
Which 'obvious' shift?

62106279
>>62106216
Its quite obvious desu

62106298
btc is going to 20k if not lower

>>62097398
>'good alts' will vastly outperform
have you been asleep for 5 years?

there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions. your odds are terrible for alts, much better chance to just throw it all on red and call it a day

62106312
>>62106298
>btc is going to 20k if not lower
>there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions
Bottom signal, the retards maxxing are back

62106435
>>62106298
Idiot

62106463
>>62097125
DCA doesn't work you stupid nigger. Just look at Michael Saylor, he's down -5% after SIX YEARS of dollar cost averaging

62107082
>>62106463

ngmi

You're not smart enough.

62108172
>>62095759
Why do you say that?

62109768
>>62097398
Sure, chase “good alts”… just don’t cry in a few years when they roundtrip to zero and BTC is sitting way higher.

You’ll wish you just held the hardest asset instead of playing ETF cosplay.

62110223
Guarantee 30% return on semiconductors and useless AI tech or gamble that Bitcoin crawls back to 100k any time soon despite losing 40% of it's value in the last few months. Hmmmm

62110242
I expected that until December Bitcoin will sunk to below 40k and Solana will be around $20. I will buy solana and Hold it until 2029 when the next bullrun will start.

2027-2028 will be crab market, everyone will say that crypto is dead just like 2023.

I will buy SOL because it will probably 20x while BTC will only 5x.

62110338
>>62102233
A 17 year old in the prime of their life.

62110351
>>62110338
Fact, can't wait to back kick some zombies

62110380
>>62087937
Why aren't you holding nano?

It literally has perfected digital money.

>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer inferior coins. What’s your reason?

62112997
>>62110380
Nano? Any major development?
